title: Control of Discharge of Oil from Machinery Space Bilges
requirement: No discharge of oily water from machinery space bilges unless: the ship is proceeding en route, oil content does not exceed 15 ppm after passing through an approved separator, and the ship is not within a Special Area.
threshold: 15 ppm oil content; minimum 12 nautical miles from nearest land outside Special Areas
equipment_required: Oil/water separator with 15 ppm alarm and automatic stopping device; Oil Discharge Monitoring Equipment (ODME) for ships ≥400 GT
record_keeping: Oil Record Book Part I — Code C (Machinery Space Operations)

title: Control of Discharge of Oil from Cargo Tank Areas of Oil Tankers
requirement: No discharge of oil or oily mixtures from cargo area of an oil tanker unless the tanker is proceeding en route, instantaneous rate of discharge does not exceed 30 litres per nautical mile, total quantity discharged does not exceed 1/30,000 of cargo, and the ship is more than 50 nm from nearest land.
threshold: 50 nautical miles from nearest land; 30 L/nm instantaneous rate; 1/30,000 of total cargo capacity
special_areas: All MARPOL Annex I Special Areas — discharge prohibited
equipment_required: Crude Oil Washing (COW) system for applicable tankers; ODME; Slop tanks
record_keeping: Oil Record Book Part II — Cargo/Ballast Operations

title: Control of Discharge of Residues of Noxious Liquid Substances
requirement: Discharge of NLS residues is controlled by category (X, Y, Z). Category X substances may not be discharged at sea at any concentration. Category Y and Z substances have specific concentration and distance thresholds.
threshold: Category X: no discharge at sea. Category Y: < 1 ppm at 12 nm from land in depth > 25m. Category Z: < 10 ppm at 12 nm
special_areas: Antarctic Area — no discharge of Category X, Y, Z substances
equipment_required: Cargo residue pump with stripping capability; Procedures and Arrangements Manual (P&A Manual)
record_keeping: Cargo Record Book for NLS tankers

title: Discharge of Sewage
requirement: No discharge of sewage into the sea except when the ship has an approved sewage treatment plant in operation, or when the ship is discharging comminuted and disinfected sewage at a distance of more than 3 nm from nearest land, or untreated sewage at more than 12 nm.
special_areas: Baltic Sea Special Area — no discharge of untreated sewage; treated sewage to meet stricter standards
equipment_required: Sewage treatment plant (STP) or comminuter/disinfection system; Sewage holding tank
record_keeping: No specific ORB required; general log entry recommended

title: Discharge of Garbage Outside Special Areas
requirement: Food wastes may be discharged beyond 12 nm from land (comminuted/ground beyond 3 nm). All other garbage (except cargo residues and cleaning agents) is prohibited from being discharged at sea. Plastics discharge is prohibited everywhere.
threshold: Food waste (comminuted): 3 nm. Food waste (not comminuted): 12 nm. All plastics: prohibited everywhere.
special_areas: All MARPOL Annex V Special Areas — only food waste > 12 nm; all other garbage prohibited
equipment_required: Garbage management plan; Incinerator (optional but common); Garbage record book
record_keeping: Garbage Record Book — entries required for each discharge or incineration

title: Discharge of Garbage Within Special Areas
requirement: Within Special Areas, only food waste may be discharged, and only beyond 12 nm from the nearest land or ice shelf. Discharge only when the ship is en route. All other garbage must be retained on board for port reception.
threshold: Food waste only: 12 nm from nearest land within Special Areas
equipment_required: Garbage management plan; Port reception facility usage records
record_keeping: Garbage Record Book Part I and II

title: Sulphur Oxides (SOx) and Particulate Matter
requirement: Fuel oil used on board shall not exceed 0.50% m/m sulphur content globally (effective 1 January 2020). Within Emission Control Areas (ECAs), sulphur content must not exceed 0.10% m/m.
equipment_required: Compliant fuel oil OR approved Exhaust Gas Cleaning System (EGCS/scrubber); Bunker Delivery Notes; Fuel Oil Record Book
record_keeping: Fuel Oil Record Book; Bunker Delivery Notes retained for 3 years

title: Nitrogen Oxides (NOx) Technical Code
requirement: Marine diesel engines installed on ships constructed on or after 1 January 2000 must comply with NOx emission limits. Tier III standards apply within NOx ECAs for engines installed on ships constructed on or after 1 January 2016.
threshold: Tier I/II: global limits. Tier III: within NOx ECAs — approximately 80% reduction from Tier I
equipment_required: NOx-compliant engine with EIAPP certificate; Engine Parameter Check Sheet; Technical File
record_keeping: Engine EIAPP certificate on board; maintenance records for NOx parameters

title: Tanks for Oil Residues (Sludge)
requirement: Every ship of 400 GT and above shall be provided with a tank of adequate capacity to retain oily residues (sludge) generated during the normal operation of the engine room.
threshold: Ships ≥ 400 GT; tank capacity per MARPOL formula based on fuel consumption and voyage duration
equipment_required: Sludge tank of adequate capacity; pumping arrangements; connection to port reception facility standard discharge connection
record_keeping: Oil Record Book Part I — Code D (Non-Automatic Discharge of Bilge Water) and Code J (Accidental Discharge)

title: Ballast Water Performance Standard
requirement: Ships must discharge ballast water that meets the D-2 performance standard: less than 10 viable organisms per m³ for organisms ≥50 micrometres, and less than 10 viable organisms per mL for organisms between 10–50 micrometres.
threshold: < 10 viable organisms/m³ (≥50µm); < 10 organisms/mL (10-50µm); specific concentrations for indicator microbes
equipment_required: Type-approved Ballast Water Treatment System (BWTS); Ballast Water Management Plan; Ballast Water Record Book
record_keeping: Ballast Water Record Book — all ballast operations recorded within 24 hours

title: Shipboard Oil Pollution Emergency Plan (SOPEP)
requirement: Every oil tanker of 150 GT and above and every other ship of 400 GT and above shall carry on board a Shipboard Oil Pollution Emergency Plan approved by the Administration.
threshold: Oil tankers ≥150 GT; other ships ≥400 GT
equipment_required: SOPEP with approved contacts and notification procedures; on-board spill response equipment as per plan
record_keeping: SOPEP must be approved; drills and exercises under the plan should be recorded in the deck log

title: Carbon Intensity Indicator (CII) Rating
requirement: From 1 January 2023, ships of 5,000 GT and above must calculate their Carbon Intensity Indicator and receive an annual CII rating (A to E). Ships rated D for three consecutive years or E for one year must submit a corrective action plan.
threshold: Ships ≥5,000 GT; annual calculation and verification; rating scale A (superior) to E (inferior)
equipment_required: Data Collection System per IMO DCS; Ship Energy Efficiency Management Plan (SEEMP) Part III
record_keeping: SEEMP Part III on board; annual CII report submitted to flag state; CII rating certificate
